generations.label: 5th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Toyota RAV4, Honda CR-V, Jeep Cherokee

known_issues.title: CVT (Lineartronic) shudder or failure
known_issues.description: The Lineartronic CVT can develop a shudder under light acceleration and, in more serious cases, fail outright, an issue significant enough that Subaru extended CVT warranty coverage and faced a related class action.
known_issues.symptoms: A shudder or vibration during light acceleration, occasional whining under load, and in severe cases a total loss of forward drive.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask if the CVT warranty extension applies to this VIN and test drive at steady highway cruise, feeling for shudder or hesitation.

known_issues.title: External head gasket coolant seep
known_issues.description: The 2.5L flat-four can develop a slow external coolant seep at the head gasket as it ages, usually visible as residue on the lower engine block.
known_issues.symptoms: A sweet coolant smell and a slowly dropping coolant level, with dried residue visible on the lower sides of the engine block.
known_issues.recommendation: Check coolant level and look underneath the engine for dried coolant residue or a sweet smell.

known_issues.title: Windshield cracking from minor road debris
known_issues.description: The windshield glass on this generation is widely reported to crack or chip more easily than average from small stones, sometimes without an obvious impact point.
known_issues.symptoms: Chips or hairline cracks in the windshield with no obvious corresponding impact damage.
known_issues.recommendation: Inspect the windshield closely for chips or cracks and ask about replacement history and whether OEM glass was used.

known_issues.title: Parasitic battery drain
known_issues.description: Some vehicles develop a parasitic electrical draw that drains the battery overnight, often traced to the infotainment or keyless entry module.
known_issues.symptoms: A dead or weak battery after the car sits for a few days, sometimes requiring a jump start even though the battery itself tests fine.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask if the vehicle has needed a jump start recently or sat unused for more than a week.

known_issues.title: Oil consumption on the 2.5L engine
known_issues.description: Some 2.5L engines consume oil above normal factory specification between changes.
known_issues.symptoms: A dipstick reading noticeably lower than expected between oil changes, with no visible leaks underneath.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask for oil consumption and top-up history, and check the dipstick before a test drive.
